_description: Company hiring guidelines derived from public sources only (no outreach). Ingest into company_hiring_criteria.json via scripts/ingest_public_company_guidelines.py. Sources: career pages, Vault/WSP/M&I, TECH_HIRING_GUIDELINES_ACCURACY, business.json.
_policy: Every entry has criteria_source (URL or citation). confidence=inferred. When we later add job feeds (e.g. Greenhouse), add a rule with that source and same company_pattern; keep this for display/source attribution.

companies.criteria_for_llm: Google values: technical depth, problem-solving, impact with scale. Resume: quantifiable impact, clean formatting, projects with real users. Interns: demonstrate learning velocity, coding ability, diversity of experience. GPA and school are factors but not the only signal.

Microsoft looks for: technical skills, growth mindset, collaboration. Students: relevant coursework, projects, internships. Engineering: systems thinking, coding, and ability to learn. Emphasize impact and teamwork.

Amazon uses Leadership Principles in hiring. Emphasize: ownership, bias for action, customer obsession, deliver results. Technical roles: depth in CS fundamentals, projects, quantifiable impact. Interns: show you can deliver and learn.
Meta values: building at scale, impact, moving fast. Engineering: strong CS fundamentals, shipped projects, systems or product impact. Emphasize technical depth and measurable outcomes.
Apple looks for: craft, attention to detail, collaboration. Technical roles: strong fundamentals, projects that show quality and impact. Interns: demonstrate curiosity and ability to ship.
Netflix values: judgment, impact, curiosity, inclusion. Senior bar for all roles. Emphasize high-impact work, ownership, and clear communication. Technical: depth and breadth, shipped systems.
Salesforce values: trust, customer success, innovation. Technical: cloud, data, integration experience helpful. Emphasize teamwork, impact, and alignment with Ohana culture.
Adobe looks for: creativity, technical skill, collaboration. Design and engineering: portfolio or projects, user impact. Emphasize craft and measurable outcomes.
IBM values: technical depth, client focus, growth mindset. Students: relevant major, projects, internships. Emphasize learning agility and impact in technical or consulting roles.
Oracle looks for: technical skills in cloud, data, or enterprise software. Students: coursework and projects in relevant areas. Emphasize problem-solving and delivery.

companies.criteria_source: goldmansachs.com/careers/students; Vault, Wall Street Prep, M&I recruiting guides
companies.criteria_for_llm: Goldman Sachs: strong GPA (3.7+ for IBD), technical modeling and deal knowledge, leadership, polish. Emphasize finance/economics/accounting coursework, Excel/modeling, quantifiable impact, and finance-related internships or investment club.

Morgan Stanley: similar bar to GS. GPA and school matter; technical modeling, professional presentation. IBD and Wealth Management pathways. Emphasize leadership, quant impact, and finance internship or club experience.
JP Morgan: broadest entry-level programs (IBD, Asset Management, Markets). GPA 3.5+ typical; quant impact, leadership, Excel/modeling. Emphasize relevant coursework and internship or club experience.
JP Morgan: broadest entry-level programs (IBD, Asset Management, Markets). GPA 3.5+ typical; quant impact, leadership, Excel/modeling. Emphasize relevant coursework and internship or club experience.

Blackstone (PE): typically recruits from 1st-year IB analysts. GPA and bank brand critical. For undergrad: emphasize top GPA, leadership, modeling, and finance internship. PE direct from undergrad is rare.
KKR (PE): recruits heavily from top IB analysts. For undergrad: exceptional GPA, modeling, leadership. Emphasize deal exposure, quant impact, and finance org involvement.
Lazard (elite boutique IB): GPA 3.7+, high deal responsibility, modeling. Emphasize technical modeling, leadership, and finance internship or investment club.
Evercore (elite boutique): similar bar to Lazard. Modeling, deal experience, leadership. Emphasize DCF/LBO, internship, and finance org leadership.

McKinsey: GPA 3.7+ for MBB, structured thinking, PEI (Personal Experience Interview). Case and behavioral rigor. Emphasize problem-solving at scale, leadership, quant/analytical projects, and case competition or consulting club.
McKinsey: GPA 3.7+ for MBB, structured thinking, PEI. Case and behavioral rigor. Emphasize problem-solving at scale, leadership, quant/analytical projects, case competition or consulting club.
BCG: GPA 3.6+, case and behavioral. Values innovation, entrepreneurial mindset. Emphasize case competition, leadership, data-driven projects. Tech + consulting backgrounds differentiating.
BCG: GPA 3.6+, case and behavioral. Values innovation, entrepreneurial mindset. Emphasize case competition, leadership, data-driven projects.
Bain: GPA 3.6+, collegial culture, strong analyst training. Case and behavioral. Emphasize case club, leadership, quant/strategy projects. High PE placement from Bain.
Bain: GPA 3.6+, case and behavioral. Emphasize case club, leadership, quant/strategy projects.

Deloitte: Strategy & Operations (S&O) is MBB-adjacent; GPA 3.5+. Consulting: case and behavioral, analytical rigor. Emphasize case competition, leadership, data or strategy projects. Good bridge to MBB lateral.
EY: EY-Parthenon for strategy; broader EY for advisory. GPA 3.5+ for strategy. Emphasize analytical projects, leadership, case or consulting club experience.
KPMG: advisory and consulting; GPA and analytical skills. Emphasize leadership, quant/analytical work, and relevant internship or club experience.
Accenture: technology and strategy consulting. Emphasize problem-solving, teamwork, technical or analytical projects. Broad entry points for students.

Oliver Wyman: Tier 2 strategy; strong in financial services. GPA 3.5+, analytical rigor. Emphasize case competition, data-driven projects, leadership. Differentiating for finance/insurance consulting.
Airbnb values: design, belonging, craft. Engineering: impact, ownership, systems thinking. Emphasize projects with real users, collaboration, and measurable outcomes.
Spotify looks for: passion for music/product, technical depth, collaboration. Emphasize shipped projects, impact, and culture fit.
Uber values: impact at scale, ownership, boldness. Technical: systems, data, product. Emphasize quantifiable impact and shipped work.
Lyft looks for: technical skill, mission alignment, teamwork. Emphasize projects, impact, and learning velocity.
Twilio values: builders, API/developer focus, ownership. Emphasize technical depth, shipped projects, and developer experience.
Datadog: observability, infrastructure, engineering. Emphasize systems thinking, metrics/impact, and technical projects.
Snowflake: data cloud, engineering bar. Emphasize data systems, scalability, and technical impact.
ServiceNow: enterprise software, platform. Emphasize technical depth, delivery, and customer impact.
Workday: enterprise HR/finance software. Emphasize technical skills, teamwork, and relevant coursework or projects.
Intuit: fintech, TurboTax, QuickBooks. Emphasize technical and product impact, customer focus, and innovation.
PayPal: fintech, payments. Emphasize technical depth, security/scale, and measurable impact.
Visa: payments, global scale. Emphasize technical or analytical projects, leadership, and impact.
Mastercard: payments, data, innovation. Emphasize technical skills, teamwork, and quantifiable impact.
